signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence + RBA
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7240 — COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) vs retail 88% short. This extreme institutional vs retail divergence occurs as RBA 4.35% (HIKING) vs Fed 3.75% (HOLD) provides a -60bp carry premium favoring AUD. Retail is aggressively fading the institutional trend and hawkish CB bias; retail capitulation risk accelerates if price clears the 100-day EMA. Carry and positioning alignment supports further upside until VIX > 25.
signals.watch level: Watch for RBA meeting on 16 Jun to confirm hiking trajectory.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 with long% at an extreme low of 18.5% (specs heavily short) vs RBNZ market bias for a Hike. NZD/USD at 0.5947 faces significant short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ delivers or signals a hike at the 27 May meeting. The 150bp Fed-NZD carry advantage currently favors USD, but crowded spec shorts are vulnerable to a hawkish shift in forward guidance.
signals.watch level: RBNZ meeting on 27 May is the primary catalyst for a short-unwind.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.15 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite Trump-Xi summit headlines introducing geopolitical noise. JPY COT LF net short -61,340 remains crowded. While VIX at 18.1 (normal volatility) keeps carry dominant, 160.00 remains the critical level for BoJ verbal warnings and potential intervention. JPY safe-haven function remains secondary to the yield gap while VIX stays below 25.
signals.watch level: 160.00 remains the key level for BoJ intervention risk.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + ECB Stance
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.715 vs norm of -0.115 (z=+1.74σ) — the pair is moving in lockstep with European equities, breaking historical norms. ECB's Kocher signals no delay in rate hikes if energy prices remain elevated, supporting EUR at 1.1770. However, the +175bp Fed-ECB carry advantage structurally limits upside potential ahead of US CPI data.
signals.watch level: Next ECB meeting on 11 Jun determines if the hawkish rhetoric shifts to policy.
